Checklist Item: Semantic-to-div ratio above threshold; pages with >70% generic containers (div/span) flagged
Comments: 219 markup files analyzed: overall 59.3% generic (div/span) vs 40.7% semantic. 47 files exceed 70% generic threshold (e.g. timeline.vue/html, searchresults placeholders). No ESLint/CI rule enforces semantic ratio.

Checklist Item: Correct heading hierarchy maintained
Comments: 157 files use headings; 17 have hierarchy violations (skipped levels e.g. h3→h6, h1→h4) or multiple h1 (manageguests modal, folderpage, storybook). No eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y heading-level rule in repo.

Checklist Item: Single h1 per page where applicable
Comments: 5 markup files contain multiple <h1> tags including production AEM components: folderpage.html, addEditGuestModal.html, activity-category-list.html. Storybook/static demos also affected.

Checklist Item: DOM source order matches visual tab order; no positive tabindex values; CSS order/flex-direction does not contradict DOM sequence
Comments: Positive tabindex found: modal-publish-view.html uses tabindex="1" on modal roundel button. Compiled CSS uses flex-direction row-reverse/column-reverse in several places; cannot verify tab vs visual order without runtime test.

Checklist Item: Page content hierarchy intact: headings and body text present in raw HTML source; critical content not injected solely via JavaScript; no content exclusively hidden via inline style or JS-only class toggling
Comments: 131+ AEM partials use placeholders/skeleton markup without headings or body text (e.g. results-placeholder.html is div-only loaders). Search/booking content is Vue-hydrated; raw HTML lacks substantive content hierarchy.

Checklist Item: Avoid excessive DOM depth
Comments: DOM depth scan of ui.apps markup: only 4 files reach depth 15–17 (basket.html max 17); vast majority of templates stay shallower. No systematic over-nesting.

Checklist Item: Radio/checkbox groups wrapped in fieldset with legend; forms with 3+ related inputs have grouping
Comments: fieldset/legend used in many forms (264 refs) but 6 templates with 3+ radio/checkbox lack fieldset, including author-view.html (26 inputs). Vue form components also omit fieldset grouping.

Checklist Item: All inputs have associated labels
Comments: Automated scan found 19 ui.apps templates and 1 Vue component with visible inputs lacking <label for> or aria-label/aria-labelledby (e.g. village-dropdown-view.html, basket.html, booking magnet forms).

Checklist Item: Placeholder not used as label
Comments: Most form inputs use explicit labels, but search-activity.html search field relies on placeholder="Search ..." with no associated <label> or aria-label.

Checklist Item: Error messages programmatically associated with inputs via aria-describedby or aria-errormessage; error containers use role='alert' or aria-live='assertive'; error text is non-empty and >10 characters
Comments: FormValidator sets aria-invalid only; no aria-describedby/aria-errormessage wiring to .js-input-error spans. Some auth flows use role=alert, but field-level errors in personal-details and most AEM forms lack programmatic association.

Checklist Item: Avoid inline styles
Comments: Only 15 inline style attributes in source markup (mostly author-view background-image previews, embed backgroundColor, clear:both). Styling overwhelmingly uses SCSS classes.

Checklist Item: Accessible names for interactive elements
Comments: 21 templates contain <button> elements with no visible text and no aria-label/aria-labelledby/title (searchbar dropdowns, modal close, date pickers, login forms).

Checklist Item: Images with role='presentation' or aria-hidden='true' have empty alt; standalone without adjacent text and without alt flagged; hero/banner images verified to have non-empty alt >10 chars; in decorative containers verified to have empty alt or role='presentation'
Comments: 4 tags lack alt and are not marked decorative (item-blocks.html, list.html, emptytextimage.html, author-placeholders.html). 4 images have alt text ≤10 chars. No decorative images with non-empty alt found.

Checklist Item: Alt text is not a filename or path; not generic (image/photo/icon/picture/graphic/banner/logo without qualifier); between 5-125 characters; does not duplicate adjacent text content; does not begin with 'image of'/'picture of'/'photo of'; alt on linked images describes destination
Comments: Most alt text is descriptive and within length limits. Violations: basket.html uses alt="spa" (3 chars, non-descriptive); author-placeholders.html has short alt. No widespread filename/generic alt patterns.

Checklist Item: Tables use thead and tbody
Comments: 7 table templates omit thead and/or tbody (costbreakdown, refundsummary, detailedreceipt, activity-costbreakdown, basket).

Checklist Item: Table headers use th
Comments: All tables in ui.apps use <th> for column headers (e.g. costbreakdown.html receipt headers). No tables found using <td> for header cells only.

Checklist Item: Lists use ul or ol correctly
Comments: Navigation and content lists predominantly use <ul>/<ol>. Standalone <li> in sub-menu-template.html are HTL partials included inside parent <ul> menus, not div-based faux lists.

Checklist Item: Page <title> exists; is >10 characters; not a default value (Untitled/Document/Home/Page/Website/Welcome); follows consistent pattern across site (e.g. 'Page - Site' or 'Page | Site' separator detected)
Comments: head.js sets <title> as pageTitle/title + ' | Center Parcs' via head.html. No default Untitled/Home placeholders found. Dynamic AEM titles exceed 10 chars when page has content.

Checklist Item: Meta viewport configured correctly
Comments: Primary page head.html includes <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"/>. XF page template also sets responsive viewport.

Checklist Item: No duplicate IDs
Comments: 64 static id values reused across templates (email×8, firstName×8, modal_desc×7). 14 files duplicate ids within the same template. Form/modal ids are not namespaced per instance.

Checklist Item: No invalid HTML nesting
Comments: Line-level scan found no nested in ,

Checklist Item: HTML validates without errors
Comments: No HTML validation step in package.json, pom.xml, or GitHub workflows. W3C/htmlhint not configured; cannot confirm zero validation errors.

Checklist Item: ARIA roles do not duplicate native semantics
Comments: Redundant ARIA on native elements: role="button" on <a> (refundsummary), role="heading" on <h2>/<h3> (basket.html), role="main" on <motion> (claimbooking, voucher-code, basket).

Checklist Item: Avoid misuse of ARIA roles
Comments: Misused roles: role="heading" on <span> (footer-component.html), role="main" on generic <motion> wrappers instead of <main>, role="navigation" on <nav> is acceptable.

Checklist Item: ARIA used only when necessary
Comments: Excessive redundant ARIA across aquasana basket/booking templates (role=heading on headings, aria-label duplicating visible text). Native HTML would suffice in most cases.

Checklist Item: Meta tags do not block zoom
Comments: Viewport meta uses width=device-width, initial-scale=1 without user-scalable=no or maximum-scale=1. No zoom-blocking meta tags found.

Checklist Item: No broken links (run broken-link-checker or linkinator CLI against local build or source HTML)
Comments: No broken-link-checker/linkinator step in CI or audit scripts. AEM dynamic hrefs prevent reliable static link validation without deployed build.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for all colors
Comments: Colors defined as Sass $ variables in 01_variables.scss ($block-primary-orange, etc.), not CSS custom properties. Only 7 var(--*) usages in SCSS; 277 hardcoded hex values remain.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for spacing scale
Comments: Spacing scale is implemented as SCSS variables ($padding-1, $padding-2, etc.) in 01_variables.scss, not as a CSS custom-properties scale. Grep of ui.frontend *.scss found no :root/--spacing token definitions. Only isolated runtime vars (e.g. var(--padding) in accommodation-date-skipper) appear; most layout spacing uses $padding-* or raw px/rem.

Checklist Item: CSS variables used for typography scale
Comments: Typography scale is defined as SCSS variables ($font-size-8 through $font-size-30, $font-size-default) in cp-font.scss with px-to-em(), not as CSS custom properties. No project-wide var(--*) typography token layer found in *.scss.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded hex/rgb color values
Comments: Large-scale use of hardcoded hex/rgb across SCSS: grep counted 771 hex/rgba matches under ui.frontend *.scss. Examples include inline colors (e.g. ReservationHero.scss) and many Storybook component SCSS variables. Requirement not met project-wide.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded pixel spacing where tokens exist
Comments: SCSS spacing tokens ($padding-* in Pattern Lab/global) exist but many components use literal px/rem for padding/margin/gap instead. Rough count: 626 *.scss lines matching pixel-based padding/margin/gap patterns under ui.frontend. Not consistently token-backed.

Checklist Item: Consistent naming conventions
Comments: Naming is not uniformly applied across stacks: AEM SCSS organized as kebab-case module folders vs Storybook PascalCase components; selectors mix strict BEM (e.g. .nav__item in primary-nav.scss) with ad hoc camelCase utility classes (.searchDropownOpen in Page.scss). SCSS vars also vary ($padding-*, $font-size-*, $background-color_*). Enough drift to fail a single-standard convention.

Checklist Item: Class names do not contain hex values or color names as identifiers; no purely positional names (left1/col2); BEM or project naming convention pattern enforced
Comments: Class names embed color semantics and presentation: e.g. .btn--blue, .btn--light-grey, .btn--search-green (button.scss), .orange/.light-blue (basket.scss), .green-hyperlink (textImage.scss), Storybook Colors.scss uses .color--white/.color--black etc. BEM-ish patterns appear in places but modifiers often encode color/position semantics rather than structure-only naming. Requirement not enforced.

Checklist Item: CSS selectors for same component co-located in same file; no duplicate selectors scattered across files; related rules grouped by component
Comments: Same component selectors appear across multiple SCSS boundaries: `.search-bar` is referenced from Pattern Lab (organism + templates/pages), AEM module files (search-bar.scss, dates, activities-searchbar), and Storybook ReservationHero.scss (10 grep hits). Structural goal of single-file/co-located selectors is not consistently met.

Checklist Item: Layout containers use display:flex or display:grid; no float:left/right for multi-column layouts; no display:table for non-tabular content; absolute positioning in <5% of layout containers
Comments: Legacy layout patterns remain widespread: 267 occurrences of float:left/right in *.scss across ui.frontend (e.g. booking-block.scss uses floats for column-like layout alongside display:table/table-cell rows). Multiple non-vendor screens use display:table/table-cell/clearfix-based table patterns (booking-block.scss, basket/cost-breakdown-bundle, AEM guest-details, category-block). position:absolute occurs 328 times vs flex/grid ~379—not evidence of rare absolute positioning. Modern flex/grid is present but criteria are not satisfied holistically.

Checklist Item: min-width media queries outnumber max-width queries; base styles (outside media queries) target mobile viewport dimensions
Comments: Mobile-first/targeting criterion not met numerically: simple token counts across ui.frontend *.scss show `max-width` appears 394 times vs `min-width` 234—max-width dominates. Mixins reinforce desktop-down patterns (see 02_mixins.scss exporting max-width based helpers used across AEM/item-card/etc.). Indicates legacy max-width breakpoints are primary, conflicting with checklist.

Checklist Item: Standard breakpoints only
Comments: Many ad hoc literal breakpoints appear outside a single shared token table: Storybook AccommodationGallery uses 834px; request-brochure uses 600/768/960; enhancement-shimmer mixes 649/650/1200/1350; Notification uses 725px; Pattern Lab global-styles uses 42em. Combined with dozens of SCSS variables ($large, $med, custom Storybook breakpoints), breakpoint usage is heterogeneous rather than a locked standard palette only.

Checklist Item: Responsive images by default
Comments: AEM Core Image-derived HTL templates (core-components/image, galleryitem) emit srcset+sizes+lasy loading. However many authored templates bypass this pattern: examples include thumbnail (uxp-commons/content/core-components/list/list.html), static marketing preview images without srcset (searchresults/author-view.html), Vue inline :src singular URLs (restaurants booking block), Storybook/front-end patterns lack systematic srcset. Not project-wide "by default".

Checklist Item: Avoid z-index escalation
Comments: z-index layering is uncontrolled escalation: audited *.scss literals include 10999–120001, quad-9 stacks (multiple 9999), plus explicit 999999 in enhancement.scss (called out even in helpers/z-index.scss commentary). Presence of arbitrarily large competing values defeats 'avoid escalation' discipline.

Checklist Item: Avoid expensive box-shadows
Comments: 68 box-shadow declarations in ui.frontend SCSS include stacked multi-layer shadows (e.g., details-page/global-styles patterns with two comma-separated shadows) and high-blur composites (credit-card molecule: blur 28px paired with secondary drop; Storybook Carousel/Expandable variants up to blur 30px). These materially increase repaint cost versus flat UI and are adopted without restraint.

Checklist Item: Avoid heavy CSS filters
Comments: CSS filters persist beyond typography tweaks: grayscale on buttons/global-styles/accepted-cards, legacy IE gradients, Owl Alpha filters—and notably heavy multi-function filters (`brightness sepia hue-rotate saturate` chain) applied in basket.scss for icon treatment. These incur compositing cost and conflict with 'avoid heavy filters' guidance.

Checklist Item: No infinite CSS animations on non-loading elements; animation duration under 1s; total animation count per page within threshold
Comments: Infinite CSS animations recur outside tight loader-only usage: shimmer skeleton utility sets `animation: shimmer 1.5s infinite linear` (helpers/animation.scss)—exceeds the <1s duration rule when applied to `.timeline-placeholder`. Same 1.5s infinite shimmer is duplicated on Storybook TickerTape loading state (`&.timeline-loading`). Additional skeleton modules (`*shimmer.scss`, facebook-placeholder) iterate infinitely. Spinner/photoswipe vendor loaders are arguably OK, but the decorative/skeleton ticker pattern violates stated constraints cumulatively.

Checklist Item: All CSS properties/values supported in target browsers (checked against caniuse compatibility data); no unprefixed experimental features without fallbacks
Comments: No repo-level evidence browsers were validated vs caniuse (no automation/config captured). Styles mix legacy IE-era filters (progid:DXImageTransform) with modern visuals, and newer properties appear without guarded fallbacks—e.g. multiple backdrop-filter blur usages in Storybook SCSS with no accompanying -webkit prefix or translucent solid fallback verified in-repo.

Checklist Item: Avoid vendor prefixes unless required
Comments: Authoring still vendors large third-party prefixed CSS into the repo (normalize, Owl/Slick carousel SCSS forks) yielding dozens of manual `-webkit-/-moz-/-ms-` occurrences even where build-time autoprefix could manage standard properties. First-party usages also carry legacy knobs (e.g. `-webkit-overflow-scrolling` in date-skipper). Overall duplication exceeds a minimal-prefix policy.

Checklist Item: No duplicated CSS rules
Comments: Identical authored declarations recur across disparate SCSS targets—same dual-layer box shadow string is copy-pasted in details-page.scss, main-search-bar-vertical.scss, and AEM details-block.scss. Parallel Pattern Lab vs AEM bundles (massive duplicated search-bar sources) amplify rule repetition. Requirement of no duplicated CSS is not upheld.

Checklist Item: No unused CSS selectors
Comments: No CSS dead-code tooling is configured around the AEM clientlibs: clientlib.generator copies entire compiled bundles (`*site*/*.css`) without PurgeCSS/coverage tooling (repo search shows no purge pipeline). Architectural Pattern Lab + global site stylesheet model ships broad selector surface per page, guaranteeing unused selectors at runtime.

Checklist Item: Consistent property ordering
Comments: Stylelint baseline extends standard Sass guidelines but `.stylelintrc.yml` does not enable any declaration-order plugin (`properties-order`/SMACSS/CSSComb analog). Repo therefore lacks automation ensuring consistent grouping (position→box-model→typography→visual). Developers can arbitrarily order declarations; no evidenced convention enforcement.

Checklist Item: No blocking scripts in head
Comments: Head partial `headlibs.html` injects synchronous third-party/marketing payloads: Launch script appended as `<script type="text/javascript" src="${...launchScriptUrl}"></script>` with no defer/async/module attribute, guaranteeing parser-blocking evaluation in head. AEM ContextHub include also precedes footer work. Blocking-by-default contradicts guideline despite async CSS loaders.

Checklist Item: Use async or defer appropriately
Comments: Global head wiring still drops classic sync script tags: Launch analytics URL is wired without `defer`/`async`, so pages cannot guarantee non-blocking JS delivery. Other clientlib includes rely on AEM helpers, but this explicit tag alone breaks the policy of consistently choosing async/defer semantics for third-party JS.

Checklist Item: Third-party scripts lazy loaded
Comments: Third-party payloads (Adobe Launch via `launchScriptUrl`) load immediately as parser-blocking script includes in `headlibs.html` with no dynamic import/idle/onload gating. Adobe Typekit CSS is render-linked but still eagerly requested. No pattern (e.g., user interaction, Partytown, requestIdleCallback loader) demonstrates systematic lazy loading of marketing/analytics vendors.

Checklist Item: No JS files loaded on pages where their exports are unused; code-split bundles match route usage
Comments: Webpack still injects jQuery globally via `ProvidePlugin` for every entry, guaranteeing unused-heavy globals on routes that never touch DOM helpers. AEM clientlib packaging copies whole `site` aggregates per template styling without tree-shaken route manifests. While alternate entries exist (booking/itinerary), there is no static proof every page restricts JS graphs to exported usage only—risk remains that large shared bundles hitchhike universally.

Checklist Item: No marketing tags in head
Comments: Adobe Launch container (`launchScriptUrl`) renders inside `headlibs.html`, i.e., still within head-adjacent global includes before body execution—classic marketing/analytics tagging surface. Requirement aims to keep marketing payloads out of head; current integration contradicts.

Checklist Item: User-facing strings in component render/template functions reference i18n keys or constants; hardcoded display strings >3 words flagged
Comments: Author mode templates embed long literal English UX copy unrelated to dictionary keys—for example basket preview markup hardcodes `'1 Spa Session and 1 Badminton session'` plus placeholders like `'Save upto'`. Numerous fallbacks `'My Basket'`, `'Held Until'` use inline strings versus i18n lookup. Matches checklist flag (>3-word literals).

Checklist Item: No binaries stored in repository
Comments: Verified with git ls-files: no tracked .jar/.war/.class/.exe/.dll or other executable/build binary artifacts; node_modules not committed. Images/fonts (355 paths) are normal web assets, not inappropriate repo binaries.

Checklist Item: Error messages follow consistent pattern (checked via regex); no raw string throws; error constants or error classes used
Comments: Grepped first-party UI under ui.frontend/src/main (excluding node_modules, dist) and other frontends: no `throw '...'` / `throw "..."` matches. Hand-written code uses `throw new Error(...)` (e.g. storybook scripts). Bundled output contains many throws from dependencies. No single project-wide error-constants module observed, but the checklist’s “no raw string throws” and use of Error objects is met for authored code.

Checklist Item: Avoid tight DOM coupling
Comments: Many components use global document.querySelector/querySelectorAll against BEM class names outside their instance root (e.g. accommodation-list.vue, accommodation-card/index.vue, site centerparcs.js, dynamicLoader.js). This couples logic to page-wide selectors and sibling regions rather than refs/$el-scoped access.

Checklist Item: Avoid forced synchronous layouts
Comments: Scroll/sticky utilities defer work with requestAnimationFrame (e.g. scroll-monitor-base.js, sticky-element.js), avoiding per-scroll synchronous layout thrashing. Layout metrics (offsetHeight, getBoundingClientRect) appear in expected places (positioning, date picker, vendor pikaday) without an obvious read→write→read loop in the spots reviewed.

Checklist Item: All setTimeout/setInterval calls have corresponding clear calls; timer count per file within threshold (max 3); no timers <100ms
Comments: Automated scan of patternlab-aem/src: several first-party setTimeout(..., 10) calls (e.g. centerparcs.js, events-site/centerparcs.js, accommodation-filter-results, personal-details-section.js) violate the <100ms rule. Multiple files exceed 3 timers/file (vendor photoswipe/jquery and some bundled library chunks). Clear pairing with clearTimeout/clearInterval not fully verified in this pass.

Checklist Item: setInterval for DOM/state checks flagged (suggest MutationObserver/IntersectionObserver/EventListener); polling intervals <1s flagged
Comments: Vendor PhotoSwipe uses setInterval to poll viewport/size state (1000ms) for legacy iOS (see photoswipe.js ~972–976). PhotoSwipe UI can register idle polling via setInterval tied to timeToIdle. First-party worker timers are long-interval token refresh, not DOM polling. The checklist disfavors setInterval-based DOM/state checks.

Checklist Item: Prefer native browser APIs
Comments: Modern Vue 3 and native DOM APIs are used widely, but jQuery is still required for Slick Slider integration (e.g. cp-list-slider.js, itinerary grid-nav.js, avatars.js via window.jQuery). Lodash-style utilities remain in several modules. This falls short of consistently preferring native APIs.

Checklist Item: Modern ES6+ syntax used
Comments: Webpack/Babel transpilation; widespread import/export, const/let, arrow functions, and Vue 3 SFCs. Spot-check shows legacy `var` in a handful of places and vendor code uses older style, but first-party code is predominantly ES6+.

Checklist Item: Avoid memory leaks
Comments: Cannot exhaustively prove zero leaks in one pass. Codebase shows intentional cleanup patterns (removeEventListener in search-bar modules, slick('destroy'), StickyElement.remove disconnecting observers/listeners, shared event-dispatcher teardown). Remaining risk in long-lived window listeners and third-party plugins is unverified but not an obvious systematic absence of teardown.

Checklist Item: Event listeners cleaned up properly
Comments: Static count over first-party JS (excluding common-libs): ~287 addEventListener vs ~44 removeEventListener. While some patterns legitimately use long-lived listeners or framework teardown, the imbalance indicates many registrations without obvious symmetric cleanup in the same modules—cleanup is not consistently verifiable from code inspection.

Checklist Item: Async logic uses async/await
Comments: First-party JS still contains substantial Promise chain usage (.then/.catch) alongside async/await (roughly more .then occurrences than async declarations in a line count). Async logic is mixed rather than standardized on async/await.

Checklist Item: Promises handled correctly
Comments: Spot checks and a coarse scan show promise chains without obvious rejection handling in several modules (e.g. search-controller.js chains ContextHubWrapperInitialize().then(...) without a .catch in-file). Not all async paths appear guarded; cannot assert all promises are handled correctly.

Checklist Item: State changes occur through defined setters/reducers/actions; no direct state object property mutations outside state management layer
Comments: Vuex is used for accommodation flows, but there is direct mutation of store state outside reducers/mutations (e.g. `vuexStore.state.isFilterChipEvent = true` in activity-search-results/index.js and activity-filter/index.js). Much state also lives in component locals. This does not meet the checklist’s “no direct state object property mutations outside state management layer.”

Checklist Item: No mutation of shared state
Comments: Same finding as state-management review: components mutate shared Vuex state fields directly (e.g. isFilterChipEvent on vuexStore.state) instead of isolated mutations/actions, which risks unintended shared coupling and violates immutability expectations for shared state.

Checklist Item: No side effects in pure functions
Comments: Codebase is largely imperative Vue/jQuery-style modules. Utility modules perform I/O and DOM side effects (e.g. accessibility, EventBus, analytics). There is no systematic discipline separating pure helpers from effectful ones; side effects routinely appear in nominally reusable functions.

Checklist Item: Consistent return types
Comments: JavaScript modules lack enforced return typing; functions often return booleans, void, or mixed shapes depending on branches (typical for this stack). No project-wide contract (e.g. TypeScript signatures on all exports) ensuring consistent return types.

Checklist Item: No global variables
Comments: Explicit global namespace usage exists (e.g. newsletter-popup.js initializes `window.centerparcs`). Many modules read browser globals (`window.location`, `window.jQuery`) as expected. The checklist’s “no global variables” is not met if interpreted to forbid attaching app state to `window`.

Checklist Item: JS scoped to component or block
Comments: While most features live in ES modules per feature folder, site entry bundles (e.g. events-site/centerparcs.js) import and initialize many unrelated components on one page scope. Runtime registration is global to the document rather than strictly isolated per AEM block/EDS fragment boundary.

Checklist Item: Consistent coding style
Comments: ESLint is configured for the Pattern Lab bundle (`.eslintrc`, `.eslintignore`), and Storybook has `eslint.config.js`. Some legacy files and vendor chunks vary, but first-party code follows a documented linted style path—not perfect uniformity, but a consistent baseline exists.

Checklist Item: Avoid deeply nested callbacks
Comments: Promise chains are not heavily nested in a single expression (no chained .then(.then matches), but procedural code reaches very high brace nesting (heuristic depth 12 in basket-controller.js), indicating deeply nested callbacks/control-flow typical of legacy JS modules.

Checklist Item: Call chain depth <=4 levels; wrapper functions that only delegate flagged; files with >80% re-exports flagged
Comments: No automated call-graph analysis was run, but modules already show very deep control-flow nesting (see basket-controller heuristic). Large orchestration files and framework-agnostic utilities make a shallow call-chain guarantee unlikely; wrapper-only files and re-export ratios were not catalogued.

Checklist Item: All external inputs are validated, sanitized, and securely handled to prevent errors, misuse, and security vulnerabilities
Comments: Multiple innerHTML assignments inject server/user-derived strings (e.g. ajax-enhancement.js assigns `data` directly; contextHubWrapper sets body/button copy from objects; payment overview builds notification HTML from text). No single validation/sanitization layer was evident in these spots—risk of XSS/misuse cannot be ruled out.

Checklist Item: No unreachable code
Comments: ESLint unreachable-code rules were not executed (no local node_modules). Spot-check of representative modules shows no obvious statements after unconditional return in the same block. Full-repo dead-code analysis not performed.

Checklist Item: No empty catch blocks
Comments: Searched first-party JS for empty single-line `catch (...) { }` patterns and a small parser for immediate empty bodies; no obvious empty catch blocks surfaced. Nested/multi-statement catches are harder to prove exhaustively.

Checklist Item: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance met
Comments: WCAG 2.1 AA cannot be certified from a static code-only spot check. Prior items already note DOM coupling and innerHTML injection patterns that often correlate with accessibility and robustness gaps. Full compliance requires page testing (not performed here per browserless scope).

Checklist Item: Announce dynamic content changes
Comments: Only a handful of `aria-live` usages appear under patternlab-aem compared to the volume of dynamic UI (AJAX enhancements, Vue results, notifications). No evidence that all dynamic content updates are announced to assistive tech.

Checklist Item: Modal/dialog elements have role='dialog' or role='alertdialog' with aria-modal='true' and aria-labelledby/aria-label; keydown handler for Escape key present in source; focusable elements exist inside dialog container
Comments: The Vue `cp-modal` implementation includes `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, `aria-labelledby` pointing at a labelled header, focusable controls, and an Escape `keyup` handler. Other overlay experiences (e.g. PhotoSwipe-based lightbox) are not verified in the thin wrapper to expose the same guarantees in project source.

Checklist Item: <video> elements have <track kind='captions'> with valid non-empty src
Comments: Searched Pattern Lab sources and `ui.apps` HTML for `<video>` tags; none found in sampled paths, so no captions tracks are missing in source. If videos are added elsewhere (CMS-authored only), this wouldn’t appear in repo HTML.

Checklist Item: <audio> elements have adjacent transcript link or aria-describedby pointing to transcript content; transcript container visible (not display:none/visibility:hidden)
Comments: No `<audio>` elements found in searched `ui.apps` and Pattern Lab source trees; transcript requirement therefore not violated in repository markup. CMS-only audio would not show up here.

Checklist Item: No focus-trap calls outside of modal/dialog components; no keydown handlers that programmatically restrict Tab key to a subset of elements on non-modal pages
Comments: `focusTrap` from `utils/accessibility.js` is used outside obvious modal components—for example accommodation filter results and site switcher flows import it, while fullscreen carousel code implements its own Tab-cycling. These may not always be paired with `role="dialog"`/`aria-modal` in the same module (e.g. filter results script has no dialog role markers in-source).

Checklist Item: Visible focus styles present
Comments: SCSS includes many explicit `:focus` rules (47 matches), but multiple components set `outline: none` (carousel, chat widget, enhancement, your-details, search-bar disabled states, booking confirmation, global sign-in, cross-sell). Several are paired with custom outlines; others risk hiding visible focus depending on state/selector—not proven globally compliant.

Checklist Item: All CSS :hover rules have corresponding :focus or :focus-visible rules on the same selectors
Comments: Coarse scan: 21 SCSS files contain `:hover` but no `:focus`/`focus-visible` strings anywhere in the file (per-file heuristic). Styling often relies on mixins in other partials, but parity between hover and focus is not demonstrated file-by-file.

Checklist Item: Visually-hidden patterns (.sr-only/.visually-hidden) use approved clip/position technique; no display:none or visibility:hidden on screen-reader-targeted text
Comments: Global `.visually-hidden` uses off-screen/1×1 absolute positioning with `overflow: hidden` (not `display:none`/`visibility:hidden`). It is not the full modern `clip`/`clip-path` recipe, but matches common accessible visually-hidden approaches.

Checklist Item: No eval() or Function(string) usage
Comments: Searched Pattern Lab JS trees (including `common-libs` and `library`) for `eval(` and `new Function(`; no matches in tracked sources. Bundler configs under the same tree also showed none in the grep scope.

Checklist Item: No document.write or document.writeln
Comments: Searched Pattern Lab source/config JS (excluding built `dist/` clientlibs) for `document.write`/`document.writeln`; no matches. Committed `dist/` artifacts may still contain third-party patterns, but authored code avoids direct `document.write`.

Checklist Item: External scripts use Subresource Integrity (SRI) where applicable
Comments: HTL includes third-party `<script>` tags (e.g. Google reCAPTCHA `api.js`) without `integrity`/`crossorigin` SRI attributes. YouTube embeds and placeholder images also reference external HTTPS URLs. SRI is therefore not consistently applied where external scripts are included.

Checklist Item: No unsanitized user input in innerHTML or dangerous sinks
Comments: Confirmed dangerous sinks: `ajax-enhancement.js` assigns AJAX response HTML directly to `innerHTML`; other modules interpolate user/content strings into `innerHTML` (e.g. context hub copy, notifications). These patterns are not systematically sanitized in code.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded API keys or secrets in source
Comments: Keyword scan of frontend/AEM app code did not reveal embedded live API tokens or AWS-style access keys in authored sources. Workflow dialogs reference `apiKey` metadata field names (configuration), not literal secret values in repo text.

Checklist Item: No credentials or tokens in URLs or query parameters
Comments: Searched frontend JS for common URL credential leaks (`password=`, `access_token` in query strings, etc.)—no obvious matches. Access tokens are sent via `Authorization: Bearer …` headers (`http.js`, `GraphQL.js`), which avoids query-string exposure in those paths.

Checklist Item: No sensitive data in client-side comments or console logs
Comments: Web worker debugging logs include the resolved access token value in a template string (`tokenWorker.js` logs `data.accessToken`). Token sync also uses console logging. These leak sensitive data to the browser console in dev-oriented code paths.

Checklist Item: No insecure storage usage (localStorage/sessionStorage/Cookies or other browser storage for secrets)
Comments: Payment-related values (`bookingsdata/activityCardToken`, transaction numbers) and other booking identifiers are read/written via `sessionStorage`; `localStorage` is used for cart/booking reference signals and ContextHub persistence mocks. Storing payment tokens (even transiently) in web storage violates the “no secrets in storage” bar for this checklist.

Checklist Item: No PII stored in cookies or browser storage without encryption or consent
Comments: Browser storage persists booking references, cart change markers, multi-buy payloads, and (in mocks) full ContextHub store snapshots. These structures can embed identifiers or transactional data; code does not implement encryption or an explicit consent gate in these modules—storage is driven by feature needs alone.

Checklist Item: No known vulnerable dependencies (npm audit / Snyk)
Comments: `npm audit` in `ui.frontend/src/main/patternlab-aem` reports multiple vulnerabilities (e.g. high severity in `@babel/plugin-transform-modules-systemjs`, `@isaacs/brace-expansion`, and dependency chains around `critical-css-webpack-plugin`/`jsdom`; private packages flagged via `axios`). Fixes not fully available without breaking upgrades.

Checklist Item: No sensitive data exposed in client-visible IDs or data attributes
Comments: Authoring markup includes a disabled password input prefilled with `value="Password1"` in `login-details.html`, exposing a sample credential-like string in HTML source. Other `data-*` attributes carry field semantics/error messages rather than live secrets, but the placeholder password value is a notable client-visible exposure.

Checklist Item: Zero lint errors (ESLint/Stylelint)
Comments: ESLint is configured, but a representative file (`search-controller.js`) already reports multiple rule violations when `--max-warnings 0` is used. Repository-wide “zero lint errors/warnings” was not achieved in the sampled run; Stylelint not executed.

Checklist Item: No unused variables
Comments: ESLint is not enforced clean across the tree in this audit. Targeted runs show stylistic issues; a full `no-unused-vars` sweep was not completed, but large legacy bundles make “zero unused variables” implausible without evidence.

Checklist Item: No unused functions
Comments: No automated dead-code elimination report was generated. The repository’s size/legacy nature and commented/disabled features (e.g. commented `focusTrap` calls) indicate unused/exported helpers likely exist without proof of removal.

Checklist Item: No unused imports
Comments: Unused imports are not globally eliminated (ESLint not run clean across all entrypoints; many modules accumulate historical imports). Without a tree-wide unused-import report, the checklist requirement is not demonstrated.

Checklist Item: No dead code paths
Comments: Large modules contain feature flags, commented alternatives, and historical branches—no evidence of systematic dead-code removal (TS `--noUnusedLocals` unavailable for most JS). Static unreachable analysis not performed.

Checklist Item: No dead code files (unreferenced modules)
Comments: No webpack/ts-prune/unimported report was generated to prove every file is reachable from an entrypoint. Given multiple legacy folders and Storybook-only artifacts, unreferenced modules may exist.

Checklist Item: No commented-out production code
Comments: Commented-out production logic appears in maintained modules (e.g. `focusTrap` calls commented in `cp-modal.js`/`VModal.vue`). Thousands of `//` lines exist in `src/js`—many are annotations, but disabled production snippets violate the strict checklist wording.

Checklist Item: No implicit globals
Comments: Modules rely on bundler scope, but numerous files reference globals like `window.jQuery`, `window.centerparcs`, and browser APIs without imports. Legacy patterns and HTL-injected globals mean implicit global dependence remains.

Checklist Item: Consistent file structure
Comments: Pattern Lab code is largely organized per feature under `src/js/<feature>/` with shared `utils`, `common-ui`, and `contextHub` folders. Some naming inconsistencies exist (e.g. typo’d folder names), but the overall layout is coherent.

Checklist Item: No duplicated constants
Comments: Class selectors and string literals (e.g. BEM class names) are repeated across many modules rather than centralized constants files. No evidence of a single source of truth for all DOM selectors—duplication is common.

Checklist Item: Variable names >2 characters (excluding loop iterators i/j/k/x/y); no single-letter variables outside loops; no anti-pattern names (temp/tmp/data/val/stuff/thing/foo/bar without qualifier); camelCase or snake_case consistent per file
Comments: Widespread use of single-letter event parameters (`function(e)`) appears throughout vendor and first-party handlers. Naming is generally camelCase, but the checklist’s prohibition on single-letter variables outside tiny loops is not met literally.

Checklist Item: Function names start with verb (get/set/is/has/should/create/update/delete/handle/render/fetch/parse/validate/transform/format/check/find/load/save/remove/add/init/build/compute/on); names >4 characters; no generic names (doStuff/handleIt/process/run without descriptive suffix)
Comments: Many exported helpers use ultra-short names (`init`, `log`, `run`) that do not meet the stated >4 character minimum; mixed verb prefixes are common but not universal. Some handlers remain anonymous lambdas tied to DOM events.

Checklist Item: Consistent naming conventions
Comments: Dominant convention is camelCase for functions/vars and SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE for many constants/selectors. Occasional kebab-case filenames and AEM naming aside, the codebase reads consistent for a large JS project.

Checklist Item: No nested ternary expressions beyond 1 level; no if/else chains >4 branches (suggest switch/object-map); boolean expressions <=3 conditions per statement; complex boolean expressions without intermediate named variable flagged
Comments: `accommodation-store.js` contains multi-level conditional expressions combining logical AND with nested ternary index math—beyond the checklist’s “no nested ternary beyond 1 level / keep boolean expressions simple” guidance.

Checklist Item: Functions with if-block wrapping >80% of function body flagged for guard-clause inversion; nested if-else >3 levels deep where outermost condition is validation/type-check flagged for early return
Comments: Several modules exhibit deep nesting and large guarded regions rather than uniform guard-clause early returns (e.g. `basket-controller.js` reached high brace depth earlier). Automated flagging/restructuring was not evidenced.

Checklist Item: Average file size under 500 LOC; functions under 40 lines; module boundaries well-defined
Comments: Multiple first-party modules exceed 500 LOC (e.g. `activity-search-results/index.js` ~1.1k lines, `contextHubBookings.js` ~1.2k, `utils/utils.js` ~1.8k). This fails the average/limits guidance without needing per-function line counts.

Checklist Item: Files have single responsibility and clear module boundaries
Comments: Several modules aggregate many unrelated concerns (e.g. `utils/utils.js` with ~1.8k lines; large ContextHub/booking orchestration files). Boundaries exist at folder level, but individual files are not consistently single-responsibility.

Checklist Item: README present
Comments: README files exist at `ui.frontend/README.md` plus module-specific docs under `patternlab-aem/README.md` and Storybook.

Checklist Item: Consistent error handling strategy
Comments: Error handling is inconsistent: some GraphQL/http layers centralize headers/tokens, while many legacy modules rely on ad-hoc try/catch or unhandled promise chains. No single application-wide error boundary or strategy is evident from static review.

Checklist Item: Errors logged meaningfully
Comments: Logging mixes `console.log`/`console.error` (including token worker) with a `Logger` helper and silent catches in places. Not all error paths funnel through structured logging.

Checklist Item: Errors fail gracefully
Comments: Without uniform error handling, some async paths can leave the UI partially updated or rely on browser default failures. Prior findings (unhandled promise chains, innerHTML from network) indicate graceful degradation is not guaranteed.

Checklist Item: Avoid magic numbers
Comments: Numeric literals appear inline for UX thresholds, timers, and layout magic (e.g. `setTimeout(..., 10)`, carousel thresholds, pixel sizes scattered in JS/SCSS). Named constants are used in places but not systematically.

Checklist Item: Constants centralized
Comments: Some constants live in `utils/constants.js` and ContextHub helpers, but many BEM selector strings and magic strings remain duplicated per-module instead of being centralized.

Checklist Item: Config-driven behavior
Comments: AEM-driven configuration exists for components, but substantial behavior is hardcoded in JS (timeouts, URLs constructed in code, feature thresholds). Not a fully config-driven front end.

Checklist Item: Shared utilities extracted
Comments: The codebase contains substantial `utils/`, `common-ui/`, and ContextHub helper layers reused across features. Extraction is imperfect (duplication remains), but shared utilities clearly exist and are imported widely.

Checklist Item: Duplicate code percentage within standard threshold
Comments: No duplicate-code analysis tool was run to quantify percentage. Qualitatively, repeated DOM selector strings, copy-pasted booking URL parsing, and parallel ContextHub patterns indicate duplication likely exceeds a strict enterprise threshold.

Checklist Item: No hardcoded environment values
Comments: Development tooling hardcodes environment endpoints (e.g. `webpack.dev.js` targets `http://localhost:4502`). Runtime code may externalize more via AEM, but authored build config still embeds environment-specific hosts.

Checklist Item: Avoid circular dependencies
Comments: No dependency graph tool (e.g. madge) was executed to prove acyclic imports. Given tight coupling between ContextHub, booking, and search modules, cycles cannot be ruled out.

Checklist Item: No stale TODO/FIXME comments; no comments referencing symbols that do not exist in scope; no commented-out code blocks
Comments: Dozens of `TODO`/`FIXME` markers remain in first-party JS/Vue (54 matches). Combined with commented-out production snippets (see earlier `focusTrap` comments), the checklist is not satisfied.

Checklist Item: commented-out code blocks >3 lines flagged; TODO/FIXME without issue-tracker reference flagged;
Comments: Large commented regions and TODOs without ticket references appear across modules (e.g. TODO in `sticky-element.js`, extensive `//` blocks). No evidence of issue-tracker IDs accompanying these notes in the scanned files.

Checklist Item: Charset meta tag (<meta charset="UTF-8">) declared in <head> before any content-producing elements; charset declaration appears within first 1024 bytes of document
Comments: Structure `head.html` partial declares `<meta charset="utf-8"/>` near the top of the head resources. Final rendered byte offset depends on dispatcher output, but source templates place charset meta in the head include as expected.

Checklist Item: Language attribute (lang) on <html> element is a valid BCP 47 tag matching page content language; lang attribute not empty or set to 'undefined'
Comments: Page shell sets `<html lang="${pageHelper.getLang}">` via `page.html`, with hreflang link tags managed from metadata. Assuming `pageHelper` supplies valid BCP-47 tags in production, the template pattern matches the requirement; runtime validation not performed.

Checklist Item: Direction attribute (dir) specified on <html> or relevant container elements for RTL language pages; value is 'ltr' or 'rtl' matching content language direction
Comments: `page.html` hardcodes `dir="ltr"` on `<html>`. For locales needing RTL, direction should be derived from language like `lang`. As authored, RTL correctness is not handled.

Checklist Item: If CSS print stylesheet (@media print) is defined, print styles hide non-essential UI elements (navigation/footer/sidebar/ads)
Comments: No `@media print` blocks were found under Pattern Lab SCSS sources, so the conditional requirement about hiding chrome in print CSS does not apply; nothing contradicts the guidance.

Checklist Item: Font-family declarations defined at :root or body level using CSS custom properties; component-level stylesheets reference font-family tokens instead of hardcoding font names
Comments: Some modules still hardcode font stacks (`open-sans`, `ff-tisa-sans-web-pro`, `centerparcs-icon-font`) instead of exclusively using shared tokens/custom properties. Many files do use `$f-family-*` variables, but uniformity is missing.

Checklist Item: Content elements have explicit width and height HTML attributes or CSS aspect-ratio set to reserve layout space and prevent CLS
Comments: Multiple HTL templates render `` with only `src`/`alt` (e.g. `page-cta.html`; author preview thumbnails in `searchresults/author-view.html`) without `width`/`height` attributes or evident CSS `aspect-ratio` pairing in the same snippet.

Checklist Item: No unused npm/yarn packages in package.json; depcheck or npm-check analysis confirms all listed dependencies are imported in source code
Comments: `depcheck` / `npm-check` was not run in this audit session, so unused dependencies cannot be confirmed absent. Large devDependency surface (Storybook, critical CSS tooling) makes unused packages plausible.

Checklist Item: No setState/dispatch/state-mutation calls inside for/while/do-while/forEach/map/reduce loops; state updates batched or moved outside iteration blocks
Comments: Automated scan for state mutations nested inside `forEach`/`map` was not completed, and Vue/Vuex codepaths frequently update reactive state while iterating collections. Without proof of batching, the strict prohibition is not demonstrated.

Checklist Item: No unused component props passed from parent to child; unused prop detection via static analysis or TypeScript strict mode
Comments: Most UI components are plain Vue SFCs/JS without TypeScript strictness or automated unused-prop detection across the whole tree. Ensuring every parent→child prop is used is not evidenced.

Checklist Item: Git repository initialized with .git directory present; remote origin configured; repository is not in detached HEAD state
Comments: Verified locally: `.git` directory exists, `origin` remote points at the GitHub repo, and `HEAD` resolves to branch `bugfix/AEM-26511` (not detached).

Checklist Item: .gitignore file present and includes standard exclusions (node_modules; dist/build output; .env files; OS files; IDE config); no build artifacts or dependency folders committed
Comments: `.gitignore` exists with `node_modules`, IDE/OS junk, and many build output paths, and `git ls-files` shows no tracked `node_modules`/patternlab `dist` bundles. However common secret patterns like `.env` are not listed in `.gitignore` (no `env` substring), falling short of the checklist’s “includes .env files” expectation.

Checklist Item: Package lock file (package-lock.json or yarn.lock) committed to repository; lock file version matches package.json dependencies; no conflicting lock files present
Comments: Committed `package-lock.json` files exist for active Node packages (`patternlab-aem`, `storybook`, `ui.frontend.aquasana`, `ui.frontend.microsites`). No `yarn.lock` was found at repo root—no conflicting dual lockfiles detected.

Checklist Item: Pre-commit hooks configured (husky; lint-staged; or lefthook); hooks run linting and formatting before each commit; .husky directory or equivalent present
Comments: No `.husky/` directory, `lefthook` config, or `husky`/`lint-staged` entries were found in the repo root `package.json` / tree. Pre-commit automation as described is not present in the workspace.

Checklist Item: Node.js version specified via engines field in package.json; version is LTS or current stable
Comments: `patternlab-aem/package.json` pins `engines.node` to `^14.15.4`, which is far below current/maintenance expectations in 2026 (active LTS is much newer). Even if builds work via volta/nvm overrides, the declared engine is not a modern LTS/stable line.

Checklist Item: Hardcoded mock data or example values left in production code (e.g. 'John Doe'; 'test@example.com'; 'Lorem ipsum'; '123 Main St'; placeholder UUIDs); regex pattern scan across source files
Comments: Regex-style scan finds authoring/template placeholders shipped in `ui.apps`: disabled password field default `Password1`, repeated `via.placeholder.com` stock images in structure/authoring templates, etc. These are mock/example values in production component sources.
